factions.name: The Ironmasks
factions.role: Extortion syndicate — toll collectors and muscle for hire
factions.description: A bandit gang turned extortion syndicate controlling Ashford's trade roads. They tax every merchant entering town, take a cut of mine payroll, and have beaten three citizens badly enough to be examples. Their boss is known as The Ledger — run by [[Maren Vosk]]. Their muscle comes from somewhere further up the chain.
factions.dm_notes: Their enforcers are contracted through [[The Shadow Wing]] — not loyal to [[Maren Vosk]] personally. If she falls, those contractors walk. The street-level collectors are mostly local men who took the work because it paid better than the mine. They will not fight to the death for an organization that stops paying them.

log.note: The Ironmasks have expanded their toll operations to cover the northern road as well as the eastern approach. Farlan Dusk counted thirty-two collectors working the roads in the past week — up from eighteen a month ago.
The party drives the Ironmask holding operation out of the Keep's outer courtyard. Six Ironmasks subdued. Two escaped toward Ashford. Durnak watches from the wall without interfering.

Consequence of The Shadow Wing (ally): The Shadow Wing moves refined ore samples through the tunnel system twice a week. The operation has been running for eight months — exactly as long as the shipping discrepancy in Edda's records.
The Ironmask street operation in Ashford is broken. The toll is ended. Remaining Ironmasks have scattered into the Greywood. Corvin Ashale's warehouse is locked from the inside.

factions.name: The Trade Compact
factions.role: Regional commercial authority — regulation and trade oversight
factions.description: The regional commercial authority that officially regulates trade in and out of Ashford. They have been paying the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] toll rather than escalating — someone inside the Compact is more deeply entangled than they've admitted. [[Edda Sorn]] is their local agent, and she suspects the entanglement too.
factions.dm_notes: Someone above [[Governor Harwick Denn]] in the Compact hierarchy is aware of the skim and has been paid to look away. The investigation [[Edda Sorn]] triggered has already reached that person's desk. They are deciding whether to silence her quietly or cut their losses before the formal inquiry widens.

log.note: Consequence of The Ironmasks (ally): The Ironmasks have expanded their toll operations to cover the northern road as well as the eastern approach. Farlan Dusk counted thirty-two collectors working the roads in the past week — up from eighteen a month ago.

Consequence of The Ironmasks (ally): The party drives the Ironmask holding operation out of the Keep's outer courtyard. Six Ironmasks subdued. Two escaped toward Ashford. Durnak watches from the wall without interfering.

Consequence of The Shadow Wing (ally): The Shadow Wing moves refined ore samples through the tunnel system twice a week. The operation has been running for eight months — exactly as long as the shipping discrepancy in Edda's records.

Consequence of The Ironmasks (ally): The Ironmask street operation in Ashford is broken. The toll is ended. Remaining Ironmasks have scattered into the Greywood. Corvin Ashale's warehouse is locked from the inside.

factions.name: Greywood Rangers
factions.role: Regional militia — road patrol and civil enforcement
factions.description: The regional militia patrolling the Greywood and the roads to and from Ashford. They are stretched thin — three rangers for thirty miles of road. [[Farlan Dusk]] (retired) and [[Sister Veyne]] (active) represent what they can currently spare for Ashford.
factions.dm_notes: [[Sister Veyne]] has been in contact with a ranger commander two towns over who will send reinforcements — but only with formal [[The Trade Compact|Trade Compact]] authorization. With that backing, the Rangers could field seven active rangers on the Ashford roads within a week.

log.note: Consequence of The Ironmasks (rival): The Ironmasks have expanded their toll operations to cover the northern road as well as the eastern approach. Farlan Dusk counted thirty-two collectors working the roads in the past week — up from eighteen a month ago.

Consequence of The Ironmasks (rival): The party drives the Ironmask holding operation out of the Keep's outer courtyard. Six Ironmasks subdued. Two escaped toward Ashford. Durnak watches from the wall without interfering.

Consequence of The Ironmasks (rival): The Ironmask street operation in Ashford is broken. The toll is ended. Remaining Ironmasks have scattered into the Greywood. Corvin Ashale's warehouse is locked from the inside.

factions.name: Stonebreaker Clan
factions.role: Orc hold — keepers of Greystone Keep, three generations strong
factions.description: Orc clan occupying Greystone Keep in the hills north of Ashford. The keep controls the upper vein entrance. [[Warchief Durnak]] has no love for [[The Ironmasks]] but also no reason to help outsiders unless properly approached.
factions.dm_notes: [[Warchief Durnak]] has been quietly tracking Ironmask movement through the hills for months. He found the lower tunnel entrance two years ago and chose not to mention it — that information has been his quiet leverage against anyone who might provoke the clan. He knows more than he has said.

log.note: Consequence of The Ironmasks (rival): The Ironmasks have expanded their toll operations to cover the northern road as well as the eastern approach. Farlan Dusk counted thirty-two collectors working the roads in the past week — up from eighteen a month ago.

Consequence of The Ironmasks (rival): The party drives the Ironmask holding operation out of the Keep's outer courtyard. Six Ironmasks subdued. Two escaped toward Ashford. Durnak watches from the wall without interfering.

Consequence of The Ironmasks (rival): The Ironmask street operation in Ashford is broken. The toll is ended. Remaining Ironmasks have scattered into the Greywood. Corvin Ashale's warehouse is locked from the inside.

factions.name: Emerald Circle
factions.role: Druidic order — ancient stewards of the Greywood
factions.description: A druidic order with ancient claim to the Greywood. They tolerate the mine because the Ashcroft Vein is below the root line. They will not tolerate the expansion [[The Ironmasks]] have been forcing on the miners. [[Thane Bergrak]] is their liaison in Ashford.
factions.dm_notes: The Circle has already voted to seal the tunnels. [[Thane Bergrak]]'s presence in Ashford is a courtesy — giving the party a chance to act before the Circle does. Their deadline is the next new moon. [[Thane Bergrak]] will not extend it, and the Circle does not care who is inside the tunnels when they move.

factions.name: The Shadow Wing
factions.role: Criminal network — rare materials smuggling, above the Ironmasks
factions.description: The organization above [[The Ironmasks]]. They move rare materials out of the Vein through an unofficial channel — bypassing [[The Trade Compact]] entirely. [[Corvin Ashale]] coordinates them locally as the Factor. (DM-only)
factions.dm_notes: [[Corvin Ashale]] is the Ashford node only. The Shadow Wing has three other active operations in the region. Burning this one alerts the others but does not stop them. The network's true leadership has never visited Ashford and cannot be traced through anything in [[Corvin Ashale]]'s warehouse.

log.note: Consequence of The Ironmasks (ally): The Ironmasks have expanded their toll operations to cover the northern road as well as the eastern approach. Farlan Dusk counted thirty-two collectors working the roads in the past week — up from eighteen a month ago.

Consequence of The Ironmasks (ally): The party drives the Ironmask holding operation out of the Keep's outer courtyard. Six Ironmasks subdued. Two escaped toward Ashford. Durnak watches from the wall without interfering.
